column6: MainUse
sound: /a/ — pure open central vowel; no consonant; hiragana script
sound: /i/ — close front unrounded vowel; no consonant; hiragana
sound: /ɯ/ — close back unrounded vowel; compressed lips; unique Japanese
sound: /e/ — mid front vowel; no consonant; hiragana
sound: /o/ — mid back rounded vowel; no consonant; hiragana
sound: /ka/ — voiceless velar stop /k/ + open vowel /a/
sound: /kʲi/ — voiceless velar /k/ palatalized before front vowel /i/
sound: /kɯ/ — voiceless velar /k/ + compressed back vowel /ɯ/
sound: /ke/ — voiceless velar stop /k/ + mid front vowel /e/
sound: /ko/ — voiceless velar stop /k/ + mid back vowel /o/
sound: /sa/ — voiceless alveolar sibilant /s/ + /a/
sound: /ʃi/ — さ-row exception: palatalized to /ʃ/; romanized 'shi' not 'si'
sound: /sɯ/ — voiceless sibilant /s/ + compressed vowel /ɯ/
sound: /se/ — voiceless sibilant /s/ + mid front /e/
sound: /so/ — voiceless sibilant /s/ + mid back /o/
sound: /ta/ — voiceless alveolar stop /t/ + /a/
sound: /tʃi/ — た-row exception: affricate /tʃ/; romanized 'chi' not 'ti'
sound: /tsɯ/ — た-row exception: affricate /ts/; romanized 'tsu' not 'tu'
sound: /te/ — voiceless alveolar stop /t/ + mid front /e/
sound: /to/ — voiceless alveolar stop /t/ + mid back /o/
sound: /na/ — voiced alveolar nasal /n/ + /a/
sound: /nʲi/ — voiced nasal /n/ palatalized before front vowel /i/
sound: /nɯ/ — voiced nasal /n/ + compressed vowel /ɯ/
sound: /ne/ — voiced alveolar nasal /n/ + mid front /e/
sound: /no/ — voiced alveolar nasal /n/ + mid back /o/
sound: /ha/ — voiceless glottal fricative /h/ + /a/; は can also read /wa/ as particle
sound: /çi/ — は-row: fricative palatalizes to /ç/ before front /i/
sound: /ɸɯ/ — は-row exception: bilabial fricative /ɸ/; no English equivalent
sound: /he/ — voiceless fricative /h/ + mid front /e/; can also read /e/ as particle
sound: /ho/ — voiceless fricative /h/ + mid back /o/
sound: /ma/ — voiced bilabial nasal /m/ + /a/
sound: /mʲi/ — voiced nasal /m/ palatalized before front /i/
sound: /mɯ/ — voiced bilabial nasal /m/ + compressed vowel /ɯ/
sound: /me/ — voiced bilabial nasal /m/ + mid front /e/
sound: /mo/ — voiced bilabial nasal /m/ + mid back /o/
sound: /ja/ — palatal glide /j/ (like 'y' in 'yes') + open vowel /a/
sound: /jɯ/ — palatal glide /j/ + compressed back vowel /ɯ/
sound: /jo/ — palatal glide /j/ + mid back vowel /o/
sound: /ra/ — voiced alveolar flap/lateral /r/; unique Japanese sound, not English r
sound: /rʲi/ — Japanese /r/ palatalized before front vowel /i/
sound: /rɯ/ — voiced alveolar flap /r/ + compressed vowel /ɯ/
sound: /re/ — voiced alveolar flap /r/ + mid front /e/
sound: /ro/ — voiced alveolar flap /r/ + mid back /o/
sound: /wa/ — labial-velar glide /w/ + open vowel /a/
sound: /o/ — modern pronunciation /o/; used exclusively as direct object particle
sound: /N/ — syllabic nasal mora; assimilates to next consonant; no vowel
sound: /a/ — same phoneme as あ; katakana script used for foreign words and emphasis
sound: /i/ — same phoneme as い; katakana used for loanwords and technical terms
sound: /ɯ/ — same phoneme as う; katakana script; compressed back vowel
sound: /e/ — same phoneme as え; katakana used for foreign-origin words
sound: /o/ — same phoneme as お; katakana used for loanwords and onomatopoeia

sound: /bo/ — voiced bilabial stop /b/ + mid back /o/
sound: /pa/ — handakuten (゜) adds bilabial burst; /p/ not in native words
sound: /pʲi/ — handakuten bilabial /p/ palatalized before front /i/
sound: /pɯ/ — handakuten bilabial /p/ + compressed vowel /ɯ/

sound: /po/ — handakuten bilabial /p/ + mid back /o/
